creator: wkwu@csie.nctu.edu.tw
text:
I found that the latex2html configure will test if the ppmtogif worded. 
The test method is to grep the output of 'ppmtogif -h'.
Unfortunely, the ppmtogif (/usr/ports/graphics/netpbm, version:9.20) 
outputs "unrecognized option `-h'". The old version of netpbm will output
"usage:  ppmtogif [-interlace] [-sort] [-map mapfile] [-transparent color] [ppmfile]".

If the configure found string "transparent" or "interlace", it makes the latex2html support gif.
Otherwize, latex2html only supports png.

Who should patch their source? netpbm or latex2html?
